query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Equipoise', 'An equal distribution of weight, even balance, equilibrium', 'The essay highlights the equipoise of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Equitable', 'Fair and just, ensuring that everyone is treated equally', 'The reviewer described the tone as equitable, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Equity', 'The quality of being fair or impartial, fairness', 'The essay highlights the equity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Equivalent', 'Equal in value, function, or meaning', '“One euro is roughly equivalent to one dollar.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Equivocal', 'Open to interpretation; having multiple possible meanings', 'The author hoped the new policy would equivocal tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Equivocate', 'To use unclear language to avoid commitment', '“The politician equivocated when asked about taxes.”', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Eradicate', 'To remove or destroy utterly', 'Critics argued that the plan might eradicate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Erasure', 'The action of erasing, deletion, obliteration', 'Scholars noted a striking erasure between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Erode', 'To wear away by abrasion, corrosion or chemical reaction', 'Over time, the community learned to erode its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Err', 'To make a mistake', 'The author hoped the new policy would err tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Errant', 'Straying from the proper course or standard', 'The essay highlights the errant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Erratic', 'Unpredictable or inconsistent', '“His erratic driving made the passengers nervous.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Erroneous', 'Containing error, mistaken, incorrect, wrong', 'What seemed erroneous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ersatz', 'Made in imitation, artificial', 'The essay highlights the ersatz of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Erudite', 'Having great knowledge or learning', '“The professor’s lecture was erudite and insightful.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Erudition', 'Knowledge acquired by study, research, etc.,  learning', 'The essay highlights the erudition of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Escalate', 'To increase (something) in extent or intensity, intensify', 'Over time, the community learned to escalate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Eschew', 'To abstain or keep away from, shun, avoid', 'The essay highlights the eschew of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Esoteric', 'Intended for or understood by only a small group', '“Quantum physics is an esoteric subject for many people.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Espionage', 'The act or practice of spying', 'Her espionage became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Espouse', 'To make one''s own, adopt or embrace, as a cause', 'Scholars noted a striking espouse between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Esprit', 'Spirit, enthusiasm', 'Her esprit became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Espy', 'To catch sight or, to perceive with the eyes, to discover', 'Over time, the community learned to espy its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Esquire', 'To attend, wait on, escort', 'Over time, the community learned to esquire its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Essence', 'The inherent nature of a thing or idea', 'Her essence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Esteem', 'High regard or respect', '“She is held in high esteem by her colleagues.”', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Estimable', 'Worthy of esteem, admirable', 'What seemed estimable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Estrange', 'To cause to feel less close or friendly, alienate', 'The author hoped the new policy would estrange tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Estuary', 'Coastal water body where ocean tides and river water merge', 'The essay highlights the estuary of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ethereal', 'Light, airy, or tenuous, delicate', 'Her ethereal became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ethnographer', 'Researchers who study cultures and societies', '“Ethnographers spent years documenting indigenous traditions.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Etho', 'Characteristic spirit of a culture, era, or community', 'Her etho became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Etymology', 'The derivation of a word, word origin', 'The essay highlights the etymology of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Eugenic', 'Of bringing about improvement in produced offspring', 'The essay highlights the eugenic of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Eulogize', 'To praise or pay homage to, especially in a formal eulogy', 'Over time, the community learned to eulogize its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Eulogy', 'A speech praising someone who has died', 'The essay highlights the eulogy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Euphemism', 'A mild word substituted for a harsh one', 'Scholars noted a striking euphemism between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Euphonious', 'Pleasant-sounding, agreeable to the ear', 'The author hoped the new policy would euphonious tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Euphony', 'A pleasing pronunciation of letters and syllables', 'The essay highlights the euphony of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Euphoria', 'A feeling of happiness, confidence, or well-being', 'Her euphoria became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Euphoric', 'Feeling of great well-being or intense feeling of happiness', 'The essay highlights the euphoric of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Eureka', 'An exclamation of triumph at a discovery', 'The essay highlights the eureka of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Euthanasia', 'The practice of painlessly killing for humane reasons', 'Scholars noted a striking euthanasia between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evacuate', 'To leave or withdraw from', 'Over time, the community learned to evacuate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evade', 'Escape or avoid by cleverness', 'Scholars noted a striking evade between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evanesce', 'To disappear into a mist or dissipate in vapor', 'The author hoped the new policy would evanesce tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evanescence', 'To disappear gradually, vanish, fade away', 'Her evanescence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evanescent', 'Fading out of sight or memory', 'What seemed evanescent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evangelical', 'Pertaining to the gospel(s) of the christian new testament', 'Over time, the community learned to evangelical its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Evangelist', 'An itinerant or special preacher, especially a revivalist', 'The essay highlights the evangelist of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
